Desert View Programs and Events

Directions to Desert View on the South Rim

Desert View is a small settlement on the South Rim located 23 miles/ 37 km east of Grand Canyon Village, near the eastern edge of Grand Canyon. Arizona State Route 64, also known as Desert View Drive, is the 23 mile (37 km) scenic road that connects Desert View with Grand Canyon Village. (Allow 40 minutes driving time).

Desert View Drive is open to all vehicles throughout the year. (Unless temporarily closed by snow).

Visitors traveling through the Navajo Nation on US 89, turn west at Cameron, Arizona and follow State Route 64, for 25 miles (40 km) to reach the park's East Entrance in about 35 minutes.

For many visitors, Desert View is their first look at Grand Canyon, offering some of the best views of the Colorado River and the canyon’s dramatic geology.

 
 

Ranger Programs and Special Events

  • All programs are free of charge.
  • Children must be accompanied by an adult on all programs.  
  • All outdoor ranger programs are subject to cancellation due to inclement weather or when lightning danger is present.  
  • Note: the 2025 Summer Sunset Talks have ended.
 

Cultural Demonstrations

Visit Desert View Watchtower during spring, summer and fall, to chat with, watch and learn from tribal artists, as they share their arts and culture. Demonstrations range from weaving, silversmithing, story telling, pottery, and more.

  • Note: Demonstrations have been suspended duing the government shutdown. Visit the Cultural Demonstration webpage for more details about the program, or check the calendar below for upcoming demonstrations.
  • Cultural Performances are presented during the month of September in the Desert View Ampitheater, or outside the watchtower. Performances include tribal members sharing their rich history through storytelling, dance, music, and other great performances.

Current Ranger Programs and Events

Attend any of these programs or events hosted across Grand Canyon National Park.

Cultural Demonstration Program

Visitors to Grand Canyon National Park have the opportunity to meet Indigenous Artists while they practice traditional and modern crafts.

Desert View Watchtower History

Modeled after Ancestral Puebloan dwellings found at Hovenweep and Mesa Verde, the Desert View Watchtower opened in 1932.
